skill.name: form-cro
skill.description: Optimize non-signup forms such as lead capture, contact, demo request, application, survey, quote, and checkout forms. Use it to improve completion rate, reduce friction, and preserve useful downstream data.
skill.summary: Audits non-signup forms and recommends conversion-focused improvements.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 11 high-risk keyword patterns, but review found they are false positives in a plain-text form optimization guide. The file contains no executable code, network behavior, credential access, prompt injection text, or data exfiltration instructions. Safe to publish with low residual risk from community-source review.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Keylogger Keyword
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The phrase "After field interaction, not keystroke" describes when form validation messages should appear. It does not request capturing keystrokes, logging input, or monitoring users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is inside the Inline Validation section and gives UX timing guidance. No code or instruction collects keystrokes.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Keyword
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The front matter description begins the skill summary for form conversion optimization. It does not mention cryptography or any algorithm implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged word appears in metadata prose, not in code. There is no cryptographic operation to evaluate.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Keyword
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The bullet "premature redesigns" is ordinary product guidance about avoiding unnecessary form redesigns. It has no relationship to cryptographic primitives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a list of form audit pitfalls. No code, hashing, encryption, or security mechanism is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Keyword
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The health-band label "Broken" identifies a low form usability score. It does not describe a broken cipher or insecure security control.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a scoring table for form usability. The suspicious term is semantic business language only.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Keyword
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The metric "Mobile vs desktop split" is analytics context for form optimization. It does not reference encryption, hashing, or weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line asks for traffic segmentation data. There is no implementation detail or security-sensitive behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Keyword
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. "Auto-format and support country codes" refers to phone number field formatting. It is unrelated to cryptographic code or algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line appears under the Phone field section. The word "codes" is benign form UX terminology.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Keyword
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. "Recommended Form Design" is an output section title for UX recommendations. It does not discuss encryption or security design.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding lines list required fields, field order, copy, and layout notes. No cryptographic material is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Keyword
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. "Mobile vs desktop traffic split" is a question about analytics segmentation. It is not a cryptographic reference.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is in a questions-to-ask checklist. It contains no code or security operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Keyword
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. "Avoid label-as-placeholder anti-pattern" is accessibility and UX guidance. It does not inspect system configuration or host information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line concerns form labels and placeholders. No system commands, device probes, or environment discovery appear.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Keyword
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The example "Invalid input" is a bad error-message example for form users. It is not a request to enumerate system input devices or inspect a host.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is error message copywriting. The phrase is ordinary validation text, not reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Keyword
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The valid-email example shows user-facing validation copy. It does not collect credentials, exfiltrate data, or perform reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a benign email-format example in documentation. There is no executable link behavior or data transfer instruction.

content.value_statement: Forms often lose qualified users because fields, copy, and validation create avoidable friction. This skill audits non-signup forms and gives prioritized changes that improve completion without weakening data quality.
actual_capabilities: Scores forms with a Form Health and Friction Index from 0 to 100.
Audits required fields, optional fields, field order, and field-level friction.
Recommends clearer labels, help text, submit copy, and validation messages.
Evaluates mobile usability, touch targets, keyboard types, and layout choices.
Creates prioritized fixes and A/B test hypotheses for form improvements.
Accounts for compliance, lead quality, and downstream workflow constraints.
limitations: It excludes signup and account registration flows by design.
It needs form details, analytics, or screenshots for precise recommendations.
It does not replace legal review for regulated or compliance-sensitive fields.
It cannot measure conversion lift without experiment or analytics data.

use_cases.title: Improve Lead Capture Forms
use_cases.description: Audit a gated content or contact form and identify fields, copy, and layout changes that reduce abandonment.

use_cases.title: Refine Demo Request Flow
use_cases.description: Balance completion rate with sales qualification needs for demo, quote, and consultation request forms.

use_cases.title: Reduce Checkout Friction
use_cases.description: Review non-account checkout forms for mobile usability, error handling, trust signals, and unnecessary data collection.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Form Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single form
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this non-signup form for conversion friction. Score it with the Form Health and Friction Index and list the top five fixes: [paste fields, copy, and context].

prompt_templates.title: Field Necessity Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Check whether each field earns its place
prompt_templates.prompt: Review each field in this form. Mark it required, optional, removable, or moved later, and explain the effect on completion and lead quality.

prompt_templates.title: Mobile Form Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this form for mobile completion. Focus on keyboard types, touch targets, field order, validation timing, and submit button behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Experiment Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this form context and analytics to create prioritized A/B test hypotheses. Include expected impact, risk, measurement plan, and constraints: [paste data].

output_examples.input: A seven-field demo request form asks for name, email, phone, company, job title, company size, and project timeline.
output: Form Health and Friction Index: 72, usable with friction.
Move phone to optional unless sales routing requires it.
Keep company size if it changes routing or qualification.
Rewrite the submit button to describe the next step, such as Request a Demo Time.

output_examples.input: A mobile quote form has two columns, placeholder-only labels, and validation after each keystroke.
output: Primary bottleneck: mobile usability and cognitive load.
Use a single-column layout with persistent labels.
Trigger validation after field interaction, not during typing.
Add clear recovery text for formatting errors.

best_practices: Provide the form type, goal, required fields, and downstream use of each field.
Include analytics such as completion rate, field drop-off, device split, and error rate when available.
Ask for recommendations that preserve legal, sales, support, and fulfillment requirements.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for signup, login, or account creation flows.
Do not remove fields only to make the form shorter if the data is operationally required.
Do not test compliance fields, core qualification fields, or many variables at once without alignment.

faq.question: What types of forms does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports lead capture, contact, demo request, application, survey, quote, and non-account checkout forms.

faq.question: Does it work for signup forms?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly excludes signup and account registration flows.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the form fields, labels, submit copy, business goal, required constraints, and analytics if available.

faq.question: Can it help with mobile forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It reviews layout, touch targets, keyboard types, autofill, validation, and submit behavior for mobile users.

faq.question: Will it recommend removing every extra field?
faq.answer: No. It weighs completion rate against lead quality, compliance, routing, and downstream workflow needs.

faq.question: Can it create test ideas?
faq.answer: Yes. It can produce A/B test hypotheses with expected outcomes and measurement guidance.
